COMPLETE INDIAN RECIPES DATABASE - 100+ DISHES
================================================================================
Each recipe includes: 10-20 detailed steps, cooking time, calories, macros,
dietary classification (vegetarian/vegan/gluten-free etc.), and serving size

SECTION 1: BREAKFAST DISHES (20 recipes)
================================================================================

1. MASALA DOSA (Fermented Rice Crepe with Potato Filling)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan option, Gluten-free
Serving: 2 dosas
Prep time: 10 min (plus 8-12 hours fermentation)
Cook time: 15 min
Total calories per serving: 350

INGREDIENTS FOR DOSA BATTER:
- 1 cup parboiled rice (idli rice)
- 1/2 cup urad dal (black gram)
- 1/4 cup poha (flattened rice)
- 1/4 tsp fenugreek seeds
- Salt to taste
- Water as needed

INGREDIENTS FOR POTATO FILLING:
- 3 medium potatoes, boiled and mashed
- 1 onion, finely sliced
- 1 green chili, chopped
- 1/2 tsp mustard seeds
- 1/2 tsp urad dal
- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder
- 10-12 curry leaves
- 2 tbsp oil
- Fresh coriander
- Salt to taste

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Rinse rice and urad dal separately until water runs clear (3-4 times each)
2. Soak rice with fenugreek seeds in 3 cups water for 6 hours
3. Soak urad dal in 2 cups water for 4 hours
4. Soak poha in water for 10 minutes before grinding
5. Grind urad dal first with minimal water until smooth and fluffy (15-20 minutes in wet grinder)
6. Grind rice with poha until slightly coarse texture (not too smooth)
7. Mix both batters together with salt, consistency should be like pancake batter
8. Ferment overnight (8-12 hours) in warm place until batter rises and becomes bubbly
9. For potato filling: Heat oil, add mustard seeds, let them splutter
10. Add urad dal and curry leaves, fry until dal turns golden
11. Add sliced onions and green chilies, sauté until translucent
12. Add turmeric powder and mashed potatoes
13. Mix well, add salt and fresh coriander, cook for 2 minutes
14. Heat a non-stick tawa (griddle) to medium-high heat
15. Sprinkle water on tawa, wipe with cloth - tawa should be hot but not smoking
16. Pour ladleful of batter in center, spread quickly in circular motion to form thin circle
17. Drizzle 1/2 tsp oil around edges
18. Cook until edges lift and bottom is golden brown (2-3 minutes)
19. Place potato filling in center of dosa
20. Fold dosa over filling, serve hot with coconut chutney and sambar

NUTRITION (per serving - 2 dosas):
- Protein: 8g
- Carbohydrates: 65g
- Fiber: 6g
- Fat: 8g
- Iron: 3mg
- Calcium: 40mg

================================================================================
2. IDLI (Steamed Rice Lentil Cakes)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-free
Serving: 4 idlis
Prep time: 10 min (plus 8-12 hours fermentation)
Cook time: 10 min
Total calories per serving: 160

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup idli rice (parboiled rice)
- 1/2 cup urad dal (whole or split black gram)
- 1/4 tsp fenugreek seeds
- Salt to taste
- Water for soaking and grinding

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Wash idli rice thoroughly 3-4 times until water runs clear
2. Soak rice in 3 cups water for 6 hours
3. Wash urad dal separately 3-4 times
4. Soak urad dal with fenugreek seeds in 2 cups water for 4 hours
5. Drain urad dal and grind in wet grinder or blender
6. Add water gradually while grinding (approx 1/2 cup total)
7. Grind until batter is smooth, fluffy, and increases in volume (15-20 minutes)
8. Test by taking small amount between fingers - should feel smooth without grit
9. Remove urad dal batter into large bowl
10. Drain rice and grind to slightly coarse texture (not completely smooth)
11. Add water gradually while grinding (approx 3/4 cup total)
12. Rice should feel like fine rava (semolina) when rubbed between fingers
13. Mix rice batter with urad dal batter thoroughly using hands
14. Add salt to taste, mix well in one direction for 2 minutes to incorporate air
15. Cover and ferment in warm place for 8-12 hours
16. Batter should rise to double, become bubbly, and smell slightly sour
17. Grease idli molds with oil or ghee
18. Pour batter into each mold, filling 3/4 full (leaves room for expansion)
19. Steam in idli steamer or pressure cooker without weight for 10 minutes
20. Check doneness: Insert toothpick - should come out clean
21. Cool for 2 minutes, remove idlis using spoon or butter knife
22. Serve hot with sambar and coconut chutney

NUTRITION (per serving - 4 idlis):
- Calories: 160
- Protein: 5g
- Carbohydrates: 32g
- Fiber: 3g
- Fat: 1g
- Iron: 2mg

================================================================================
3. POHA (Flattened Rice with Vegetables)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan option
Serving: 1 bowl
Prep time: 10 min
Cook time: 10 min
Total calories per serving: 250

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup thick poha (flattened rice)
- 1 medium onion, finely chopped
- 1 potato, boiled and cubed
- 1/4 cup green peas
- 1 green chili, chopped
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp turmeric powder
- 10-12 curry leaves
- 2 tbsp peanuts
- 2 tbsp oil
- Fresh coriander, chopped
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- Salt to taste

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Place poha in colander and rinse under running water for 30 seconds
2. Do not soak or over-wash - poha should become soft but not mushy
3. Let poha drain completely for 5-10 minutes
4. Add turmeric powder and salt to drained poha, mix gently with fingers
5. Heat oil in kadai (wok) over medium heat
6. Add peanuts, fry until golden brown (2 minutes), remove and set aside
7. In same oil, add mustard seeds, let them splutter (30 seconds)
8. Add curry leaves and green chilies, fry for 30 seconds
9. Add chopped onions, sauté until translucent (2-3 minutes)
10. Add boiled potato cubes and green peas, cook for 2 minutes
11. Add the prepared poha, mix gently without breaking poha flakes
12. Cook for 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally
13. Add roasted peanuts and mix well
14. Turn off heat, add lemon juice and fresh coriander
15. Cover and let rest for 2 minutes before serving
16. Serve hot with sev (crispy chickpea noodles) on top

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 250
- Protein: 6g
- Carbohydrates: 42g
- Fiber: 5g
- Fat: 7g
- Iron: 4mg

================================================================================
4. UPMA (Semolina Porridge)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan option
Serving: 1 bowl
Prep time: 5 min
Cook time: 15 min
Total calories per serving: 280

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup semolina (sooji/rava)
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 1 green chili, chopped
- 1/2 inch ginger, grated
- 1/4 cup mixed vegetables (carrots, peas, beans)
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp urad dal
- 10-12 curry leaves
- 2 tbsp ghee or oil
- 2 cups hot water
- Fresh coriander
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- Salt to taste

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Dry roast semolina in a pan over medium heat until fragrant (3-4 minutes)
2. Semolina should turn light golden - do not brown too much
3. Remove roasted semolina to a plate
4. Heat ghee in same pan, add mustard seeds and let them splutter
5. Add urad dal and curry leaves, fry until dal is golden
6. Add chopped onions, ginger, green chilies, sauté until onions are translucent
7. Add mixed vegetables, sauté for 2 minutes
8. Add 2 cups hot water and salt, bring to boil
9. Reduce heat to low, slowly add roasted semolina while stirring continuously
10. Stir constantly to prevent lumps formation (2-3 minutes)
11. Cover and cook on low heat for 3-4 minutes
12. Semolina will absorb water and become fluffy
13. Add lemon juice and mix gently
14. Garnish with fresh coriander
15. Serve hot with coconut chutney or pickle

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 280
- Protein: 7g
- Carbohydrates: 48g
- Fiber: 4g
- Fat: 7g
- Iron: 3mg

================================================================================
5. ALOO PARATHA (Stuffed Potato Flatbread)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian
Serving: 2 parathas
Prep time: 20 min
Cook time: 20 min
Total calories per serving: 450

INGREDIENTS FOR DOUGH:
- 2 cups whole wheat flour (atta)
- 1/2 tsp salt
- Water for kneading
- 1 tbsp oil

INGREDIENTS FOR FILLING:
- 3 medium potatoes, boiled and mashed
- 1 green chili, finely chopped
- 1/2 inch ginger, grated
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1 tsp amchur (dry mango powder)
- 1 tsp garam masala
- Fresh coriander, chopped
- Salt to taste

FOR COOKING:
- Ghee or oil for roasting

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Mix whole wheat flour with salt and oil in a large bowl
2. Add water gradually and knead into soft, pliable dough (8-10 minutes)
3. Cover dough with damp cloth and rest for 15-20 minutes
4. For filling: Mash boiled potatoes thoroughly without lumps
5. Add green chili, ginger, cumin seeds, red chili powder
6. Add amchur, garam masala, salt, and fresh coriander
7. Mix everything well, divide filling into 6 equal portions
8. Divide rested dough into 6 equal balls, slightly larger than filling balls
9. Roll one dough ball into 4-inch circle using dry flour
10. Place one filling ball in center
11. Gather edges of dough to completely cover filling, pinch to seal
12. Flatten stuffed ball slightly, dust with dry flour
13. Roll gently into 7-8 inch circle (don't let filling come out)
14. Heat tawa (griddle) over medium-high heat
15. Place rolled paratha on hot tawa, cook for 30 seconds
16. Flip when small bubbles appear, cook other side for 30 seconds
17. Apply ghee or oil on top side, flip and press with spatula
18. Apply ghee on other side, flip again, press until golden brown spots appear
19. Each paratha takes about 2-3 minutes total cooking time
20. Serve hot with yogurt, pickle, or butter

NUTRITION (per serving - 2 parathas):
- Calories: 450
- Protein: 12g
- Carbohydrates: 72g
- Fiber: 10g
- Fat: 14g
- Iron: 4mg

================================================================================
6. CHILLA (Savory Chickpea Crepe)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-free
Serving: 2 chillas
Prep time: 10 min (plus 30 min resting)
Cook time: 10 min
Total calories per serving: 220

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup besan (chickpea flour)
- 1/4 cup finely chopped onion
- 1/4 cup finely chopped tomato
- 1 green chili, finely chopped
- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder
- 1/2 tsp red chili powder
- 1/2 tsp ajwain (carom seeds)
- Fresh coriander, chopped
- Salt to taste
- Water as needed
- Oil for cooking

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. In large bowl, add besan, turmeric, red chili powder, ajwain, and salt
2. Add water gradually while whisking to avoid lumps (approx 1 cup water)
3. Batter should be like dosa batter - pouring consistency (not too thick or thin)
4. Let batter rest for 15-30 minutes (allows besan to absorb water)
5. Add chopped onions, tomatoes, green chili, and coriander to batter
6. Mix well, adjust salt if needed
7. Heat non-stick tawa over medium heat
8. Lightly grease tawa with oil
9. Pour ladleful of batter onto center of tawa
10. Spread quickly in circular motion to form 7-8 inch circle
11. Drizzle 1/2 tsp oil around edges
12. Cook until edges start leaving tawa (2-3 minutes)
13. Flip chilla using spatula, cook other side for 1-2 minutes
14. Both sides should be golden brown with crispy edges
15. Remove from tawa, fold like a wrap
16. Serve hot with green chutney or ketchup

NUTRITION (per serving - 2 chillas):
- Calories: 220
- Protein: 10g
- Carbohydrates: 28g
- Fiber: 6g
- Fat: 8g
- Iron: 3mg

================================================================================
7. PONGAL (South Indian Rice Lentil Porridge)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan option
Serving: 1 bowl
Prep time: 5 min
Cook time: 25 min
Total calories per serving: 320

INGREDIENTS:
- 1/2 cup raw rice
- 1/4 cup moong dal (split yellow gram)
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp black peppercorns
- 1/2 inch ginger, grated
- 10-12 curry leaves
- 2 tbsp ghee or oil
- 1/4 cup cashew nuts
- 1/4 tsp asafoetida (hing)
- Salt to taste
- 4 cups water

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Dry roast moong dal in pan until fragrant and light golden (2-3 minutes)
2. Wash rice and roasted dal together until water runs clear
3. In pressure cooker, add rice, dal, and 4 cups water
4. Add salt, pressure cook for 3-4 whistles (about 10 minutes)
5. Let pressure release naturally
6. Meanwhile, dry roast cumin seeds and peppercorns until aromatic (1 minute)
7. Grind roasted spices into coarse powder
8. Heat ghee in small pan, add cashew nuts, fry until golden (2 minutes)
9. Add curry leaves, ginger, and asafoetida, fry for 30 seconds
10. Open pressure cooker, mash the rice-dal mixture slightly
11. Mixture should be soft and porridge-like (add more water if too thick)
12. Add ground cumin-pepper powder and mix well
13. Add the tempered ghee with cashews and curry leaves
14. Mix thoroughly, cook on low heat for 2-3 minutes
15. Serve hot with coconut chutney and sambar

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 320
- Protein: 10g
- Carbohydrates: 48g
- Fiber: 5g
- Fat: 10g
- Iron: 3mg

================================================================================
8. BREAD PAKORA (Bread Fritters)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian
Serving: 4 pieces
Prep time: 10 min
Cook time: 15 min
Total calories per serving: 280

INGREDIENTS:
- 4 slices bread
- 1 cup besan (chickpea flour)
- 1/4 tsp turmeric powder
- 1/2 tsp red chili powder
- 1/2 tsp ajwain (carom seeds)
- Pinch of baking soda
- Salt to taste
- Water for batter
- Oil for deep frying
- Tomato ketchup or green chutney for serving

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Cut each bread slice into 4 small triangles or squares
2. In bowl, mix besan, turmeric, red chili powder, ajwain, baking soda, and salt
3. Add water gradually to make thick, smooth batter (like pancake batter)
4. Batter should coat back of spoon - not too thin or thick
5. Heat oil in kadai for deep frying (medium heat)
6. Test oil: Drop small batter drop - should rise slowly, not burn
7. Dip each bread piece in batter, coat completely
8. Gently slide into hot oil
9. Fry in batches of 4-5 pieces, don't overcrowd
10. Fry until golden brown on all sides (2-3 minutes)
11. Turn occasionally for even cooking
12. Remove with slotted spoon, drain on paper towel
13. Repeat with remaining bread pieces
14. Serve hot with green chutney or tomato ketchup
15. Best enjoyed as tea-time snack

NUTRITION (per serving - 4 pieces):
- Calories: 280
- Protein: 8g
- Carbohydrates: 35g
- Fiber: 5g
- Fat: 12g
- Iron: 2mg

================================================================================
9. SABUDANA KHICHDI (Tapioca Pearl Pilaf - Fasting Food)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-free
Serving: 1 bowl
Prep time: 4 hours soaking
Cook time: 15 min
Total calories per serving: 350

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup sabudana (tapioca pearls)
- 2 medium potatoes, boiled and cubed
- 1/2 cup peanuts, roasted and coarsely crushed
- 1 green chili, chopped
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tbsp ghee or oil
- 1 tbsp sugar
- Fresh coriander
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- Rock salt (sendha namak) to taste

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Wash sabudana thoroughly in water (2-3 times)
2. Soak sabudana in water just enough to cover, for 4-5 hours
3. Sabudana is ready when pearls are soft and mash easily between fingers
4. Drain excess water completely using colander
5. Spread soaked sabudana on cloth to remove extra moisture (important!)
6. Heat ghee in pan, add cumin seeds, let them splutter
7. Add chopped green chilies, sauté for 30 seconds
8. Add boiled potato cubes, fry until light golden (3-4 minutes)
9. Add drained sabudana, mix gently
10. Cook on low-medium heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally
11. Sabudana becomes translucent when cooked
12. Add crushed peanuts, rock salt, and sugar
13. Mix well, cook for another 2-3 minutes
14. Turn off heat, add lemon juice and fresh coriander
15. Serve hot, garnish with more crushed peanuts if desired

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 350
- Protein: 6g
- Carbohydrates: 65g
- Fiber: 4g
- Fat: 8g
- Iron: 2mg

================================================================================
10. RAGI (Finger Millet) MUDDE
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-free
Serving: 2 muddes
Prep time: 5 min
Cook time: 10 min
Total calories per serving: 200

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup ragi flour (finger millet flour)
- 2 cups water
- Salt to taste
- 1 tsp ghee or oil (optional)

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Boil 2 cups water in deep pan or kadai
2. Add salt to boiling water
3. Reduce heat to low, add ragi flour slowly while stirring continuously
4. Stir vigorously with wooden spoon to prevent lumps
5. Continue stirring until mixture thickens and leaves sides of pan (3-4 minutes)
6. Mixture will form a solid dough-like mass
7. Cover and cook on low heat for 2-3 minutes
8. Turn off heat, let cool slightly until handleable (1-2 minutes)
9. Wet hands with cold water (prevents sticking)
10. Take portion of hot ragi mixture, shape into round ball (mudde)
11. Roll between palms to make smooth, firm ball
12. Make 4-5 medium sized muddes
13. Serve hot with sambar, saaru (rasam), or non-vegetarian curry
14. Traditional way: Dip mudde in curry, swallow without chewing
15. Ragi mudde is extremely healthy, high in calcium and fiber

NUTRITION (per serving - 2 muddes):
- Calories: 200
- Protein: 6g
- Carbohydrates: 42g
- Fiber: 8g
- Fat: 2g
- Calcium: 350mg (35% DV)
- Iron: 4mg

SECTION 2: LUNCH/DINNER - VEGETARIAN CURRIES (25 recipes)
================================================================================

11. PANEER BUTTER MASALA (Creamy Cottage Cheese Curry)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian
Serving: 2 bowls
Prep time: 15 min
Cook time: 25 min
Total calories per serving: 450

INGREDIENTS:
- 200g paneer (cottage cheese), cubed
- 2 large tomatoes, pureed
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1/4 cup cashew nuts, soaked and ground to paste
- 2 tbsp butter
- 1 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp coriander powder
- 1/2 tsp garam masala
- 1/4 tsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves)
- 1/4 cup heavy cream
- 1 tsp sugar
- Salt to taste
- Fresh coriander for garnish

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Heat 1 tbsp butter and oil in pan
2. Add chopped onions, sauté until golden brown (5-6 minutes)
3. Add ginger-garlic paste, sauté for 1 minute until raw smell disappears
4. Add tomato puree, cook until oil separates (7-8 minutes)
5. Add red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder, cook for 1 minute
6. Add cashew paste, mix well, cook for 2-3 minutes
7. Add 1 cup water, bring to simmer
8. Blend the gravy with immersion blender until smooth (or cool and blend in mixer)
9. Strain gravy back to pan (optional - for restaurant style smoothness)
10. Bring gravy to simmer, add salt and sugar
11. In separate pan, heat remaining butter, lightly fry paneer cubes until golden (2 minutes)
12. Add fried paneer to gravy, simmer for 3-4 minutes
13. Add garam masala and kasuri methi (crush between palms)
14. Stir in cream, mix gently
15. Simmer for 2 minutes until gravy thickens
16. Garnish with fresh coriander
17. Serve hot with naan, roti, or rice

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 450
- Protein: 18g
- Carbohydrates: 18g
- Fiber: 4g
- Fat: 35g
- Calcium: 400mg
- Iron: 2mg

================================================================================
12. DAL MAKHANI (Black Lentil Curry)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Gluten-free
Serving: 2 bowls
Prep time: 8 hours soaking
Cook time: 45 min (pressure cooker)
Total calories per serving: 350

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup whole urad dal (black gram)
- 1/4 cup rajma (kidney beans)
- 1 large onion, finely chopped
- 2 tomatoes, pureed
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 tbsp butter
- 1 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp coriander powder
- 1/2 tsp garam masala
- 1/4 cup fresh cream
- 1 tsp kasuri methi
- Salt to taste
- Fresh coriander for garnish

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Soak urad dal and rajma together in water for 8 hours or overnight
2. Drain and pressure cook with 4 cups water and salt for 6-8 whistles
3. Dals should be completely soft and mushy (about 30 minutes)
4. Mash dal slightly with back of spoon or whisk
5. Heat butter and oil in pan, add chopped onions
6. Sauté onions until deep golden brown (8-10 minutes)
7. Add ginger-garlic paste, sauté for 1 minute
8. Add tomato puree, cook until oil separates (5-6 minutes)
9. Add red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder, cook for 1 minute
10. Add cooked dal mixture to the pan, mix well
11. Add 1 cup water, bring to simmer (adjust consistency)
12. Simmer on low heat for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally
13. Dal will thicken and become creamy
14. Add garam masala and crushed kasuri methi
15. Stir in fresh cream, mix gently
16. Simmer for 5 more minutes
17. Garnish with fresh coriander and a dollop of butter
18. Serve hot with naan, roti, or jeera rice

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 350
- Protein: 16g
- Carbohydrates: 38g
- Fiber: 12g
- Fat: 16g
- Iron: 5mg
- Calcium: 80mg

================================================================================
13. CHANA MASALA (Spicy Chickpea Curry)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-free
Serving: 2 bowls
Prep time: 8 hours soaking
Cook time: 30 min
Total calories per serving: 280

INGREDIENTS:
- 1.5 cups chickpeas (chana/kabuli chana), soaked overnight
- 2 onions, finely chopped
- 2 tomatoes, pureed
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 green chilies, slit
- 2 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1 tsp turmeric powder
- 2 tsp coriander powder
- 1 tsp cumin powder
- 1 tsp amchur (dry mango powder) or 1 tbsp lemon juice
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 1 tea bag (for color - optional)
- Fresh coriander
- Salt to taste

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Pressure cook soaked chickpeas with 3 cups water, salt, and tea bag (for color) for 5-6 whistles
2. Chickpeas should be soft but not mushy (about 20 minutes)
3. Drain chickpeas, reserve cooking water, discard tea bag
4. Heat oil in pan, add cumin seeds, let them splutter
5. Add chopped onions, sauté until golden brown (8-10 minutes)
6. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, sauté for 1 minute
7. Add tomato puree, cook until oil separates (5-6 minutes)
8. Add red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder, cumin powder
9. Cook for 1 minute, add 1/4 cup water to prevent burning
10. Add cooked chickpeas, mix well
11. Add 1 cup of reserved chickpea cooking water
12. Simmer for 10-15 minutes, mashing some chickpeas with spatula
13. Gravy should thicken and chickpeas absorb flavors
14. Add amchur (or lemon juice), garam masala, and salt
15. Simmer for 5 more minutes
16. Garnish with fresh coriander and ginger juliennes
17. Serve hot with bhatura, poori, roti, or rice

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 280
- Protein: 12g
- Carbohydrates: 38g
- Fiber: 10g
- Fat: 9g
- Iron: 4mg
- Calcium: 80mg

================================================================================
14. PALAK PANEER (Spinach Cottage Cheese Curry)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Gluten-free
Serving: 2 bowls
Prep time: 15 min
Cook time: 20 min
Total calories per serving: 320

INGREDIENTS:
- 200g paneer, cubed
- 500g fresh spinach (palak)
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 2 tomatoes, pureed
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 green chilies
- 2 tbsp oil or ghee
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp coriander powder
- 1/2 tsp garam masala
- 2 tbsp cream (optional)
- Salt to taste

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Wash spinach thoroughly in multiple changes of water
2. Blanch spinach: Boil water in large pot, add spinach leaves
3. Cook for 2 minutes until spinach wilts completely
4. Drain and immediately transfer to ice water (preserves green color)
5. Blend blanched spinach with green chilies to smooth puree
6. Heat oil in pan, add cumin seeds, let them splutter
7. Add chopped onions, sauté until golden brown (5-6 minutes)
8. Add ginger-garlic paste, sauté for 1 minute
9. Add tomato puree, cook until oil separates (4-5 minutes)
10. Add red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder, cook for 1 minute
11. Add spinach puree, mix well, cook for 3-4 minutes
12. Add 1/2 cup water, salt, simmer for 5 minutes
13. In separate pan, lightly fry paneer cubes until golden (2 minutes)
14. Add fried paneer to spinach gravy
15. Add garam masala and cream (if using), mix gently
16. Simmer for 3-4 minutes for paneer to absorb flavors
17. Serve hot with roti, naan, or jeera rice

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 320
- Protein: 16g
- Carbohydrates: 14g
- Fiber: 5g
- Fat: 24g
- Iron: 6mg
- Calcium: 400mg

================================================================================
15. BHINDI MASALA (Okra Curry)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-free
Serving: 2 bowls
Prep time: 10 min
Cook time: 20 min
Total calories per serving: 180

INGREDIENTS:
- 500g bhindi (okra/ladyfinger)
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 2 tomatoes, finely chopped
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp coriander powder
- 1 tsp amchur (dry mango powder)
- 2 tbsp oil
- Fresh coriander
- Salt to taste

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Wash bhindi and dry completely with kitchen towel (very important!)
2. Cut off head and tail of each bhindi
3. Slit each bhindi lengthwise (or cut into 1-inch pieces)
4. Heat oil in pan, add cumin seeds, let them splutter
5. Add chopped onions, sauté until golden brown (5-6 minutes)
6. Add ginger-garlic paste, sauté for 1 minute
7. Add chopped tomatoes, cook until soft and mushy (4-5 minutes)
8. Add red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder
9. Cook masala for 2-3 minutes until oil separates
10. Add bhindi, mix gently (don't break okra)
11. Cook on medium heat without covering (prevents sliminess)
12. Stir occasionally, cook for 8-10 minutes
13. Bhindi will shrink and become crispy
14. Add amchur and salt, mix well
15. Cook for 2-3 more minutes
16. Garnish with fresh coriander
17. Serve hot with roti or paratha

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 180
- Protein: 4g
- Carbohydrates: 18g
- Fiber: 6g
- Fat: 11g
- Iron: 2mg
- Calcium: 100mg

================================================================================
16. BAINGAN BHARTA (Roasted Eggplant Mash)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-free
Serving: 2 bowls
Prep time: 15 min
Cook time: 25 min
Total calories per serving: 200

INGREDIENTS:
- 2 large eggplants (baingan)
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 2 tomatoes, finely chopped
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 green chilies, chopped
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp coriander powder
- 2 tbsp oil
- Fresh coriander
- Salt to taste

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Wash eggplants, apply oil on skin
2. Roast eggplants directly on gas flame (or grill in oven)
3. Turn frequently using tongs until skin is charred and blistered (10-12 minutes)
4. Eggplant should feel soft and cooked inside
5. Let cool completely, then peel off charred skin
6. Mash roasted eggplant pulp with fork or hands
7. Heat oil in pan, add cumin seeds, let them splutter
8. Add chopped onions, sauté until golden brown (5-6 minutes)
9. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, sauté for 1 minute
10. Add chopped tomatoes, cook until soft and mushy (4-5 minutes)
11. Add red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder
12. Cook masala for 2-3 minutes until oil separates
13. Add mashed eggplant, mix well
14. Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally
15. Add salt, mix well, cook for 3-4 minutes
16. Garnish with fresh coriander
17. Serve hot with roti, naan, or paratha

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 200
- Protein: 4g
- Carbohydrates: 18g
- Fiber: 8g
- Fat: 14g
- Iron: 2mg
- Calcium: 40mg

================================================================================
17. ALOO GOBI (Potato Cauliflower Curry)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-free
Serving: 2 bowls
Prep time: 10 min
Cook time: 20 min
Total calories per serving: 220

INGREDIENTS:
- 2 potatoes, cubed
- 1 cauliflower, cut into florets
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 2 tomatoes, finely chopped
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp coriander powder
- 1/2 tsp garam masala
- 2 tbsp oil
- Fresh coriander
- Salt to taste

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Heat oil in pan, add cumin seeds, let them splutter
2. Add chopped onions, sauté until golden brown (5-6 minutes)
3. Add ginger-garlic paste, sauté for 1 minute
4. Add chopped tomatoes, cook until soft and mushy (4-5 minutes)
5. Add red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder
6. Cook masala for 2-3 minutes until oil separates
7. Add potato cubes, mix well, cook for 2 minutes
8. Add cauliflower florets, mix gently
9. Add 1/2 cup water and salt, bring to simmer
10. Cover and cook on medium heat for 10-12 minutes
11. Stir occasionally to prevent burning
12. Vegetables should be soft but not mushy
13. Add garam masala, mix well
14. Cook uncovered for 3-4 minutes to dry excess water
15. Garnish with fresh coriander
16. Serve hot with roti, paratha, or rice

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 220
- Protein: 6g
- Carbohydrates: 28g
- Fiber: 6g

18. MATAR PANEER (Peas Cottage Cheese Curry)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Gluten-free
Serving: 2 bowls
Prep time: 10 min
Cook time: 20 min
Total calories per serving: 350

INGREDIENTS:
- 200g paneer, cubed
- 1 cup green peas (fresh or frozen)

- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 tbsp oil or ghee

- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Heat oil in pan, add cumin seeds, let them splutter
2. Add chopped onions, sauté until golden brown (8-10 minutes)
3. Add ginger-garlic paste, sauté for 1 minute
4. Add tomato puree, cook until oil separates (5-6 minutes)
5. Add red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder
6. Cook masala for 2 minutes
7. Add green peas, mix well, cook for 2 minutes
8. Add 1 cup water, bring to simmer
9. Add salt, cook for 5-6 minutes
10. In separate pan, lightly fry paneer cubes until golden (2 minutes)
11. Add fried paneer to gravy
12. Simmer for 5 minutes
13. Add garam masala and crushed kasuri methi

19. KADHI PAKODA (Yogurt Curry with Chickpea Dumplings)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Gluten-free option
Serving: 2 bowls
Prep time: 15 min
Cook time: 30 min
Total calories per serving: 380

INGREDIENTS FOR KADHI:
- 1 cup sour yogurt
- 2 tbsp besan (chickpea flour)
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 10-12 curry leaves
- 2 dried red chilies
- 2 tbsp oil or ghee
- Salt to taste
- 4 cups water

INGREDIENTS FOR PAKODA:
- 1 cup besan
- 1 onion, finely sliced
- 1 green chili, chopped
- 1/4 tsp ajwain
- Pinch of baking soda
- Salt to taste
- Oil for deep frying

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS FOR PAKODA:
1. Mix besan with sliced onion, green chili, ajwain, baking soda, and salt
2. Add water gradually to make thick batter (like pakoda consistency)
3. Heat oil in kadai for deep frying
4. Drop small portions of batter into hot oil
5. Fry pakodas until golden brown (3-4 minutes)
6. Remove and drain on paper towel

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS FOR KADHI:
1. In bowl, whisk yogurt until smooth
2. Add besan, whisk to remove lumps
3. Add 2 cups water, whisk well to make smooth mixture
4. Heat oil in large pan, add cumin seeds, mustard seeds
5. Let seeds splutter, add curry leaves and dried red chilies
6. Add chopped onions, sauté until golden (5-6 minutes)
7. Add ginger-garlic paste, sauté for 1 minute
8. Add turmeric and red chili powder, sauté for 30 seconds
9. Reduce heat to low, add yogurt-besan mixture
10. Stir continuously to prevent curdling (3-4 minutes)
11. Add remaining 2 cups water and salt
12. Bring to simmer, stirring occasionally
13. Kadhi will thicken as it cooks (15-20 minutes)
14. Add fried pakodas to kadhi, simmer for 5 minutes
15. Garnish with fresh coriander
16. Serve hot with steamed rice

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 380
- Protein: 14g
- Carbohydrates: 40g
- Fiber: 6g
- Fat: 20g
- Calcium: 200mg
- Iron: 3mg

================================================================================
20. MALAI KOFTA (Vegetable Dumplings in Creamy Gravy)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian
Serving: 2 servings (6 koftas)
Prep time: 30 min
Cook time: 30 min
Total calories per serving: 550

INGREDIENTS FOR KOFTA:
- 2 potatoes, boiled and mashed
- 1/2 cup paneer, grated
- 1/4 cup mixed vegetables (carrots, peas, beans), finely chopped
- 2 tbsp corn flour
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1/2 tsp garam masala
- 1/2 cup raisins and cashews (optional filling)
- Oil for deep frying

INGREDIENTS FOR GRAVY:
- 2 onions, finely chopped
- 4 tomatoes, pureed
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1/4 cup cashew nuts, soaked and ground to paste
- 2 tbsp oil or ghee
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp coriander powder
- 1/2 tsp garam masala
- 1/4 cup cream
- 1 tsp kasuri methi
- Salt to taste
- Fresh coriander

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS FOR KOFTA:
1. Mix mashed potatoes, grated paneer, vegetables, corn flour
2. Add red chili powder, garam masala, salt, mix well
3. Divide mixture into 10-12 equal portions
4. Flatten each portion, place raisin and cashew in center if using
5. Roll into smooth balls (koftas)
6. Heat oil for deep frying
7. Fry koftas on medium heat until golden brown (4-5 minutes)
8. Drain on paper towel

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS FOR GRAVY:
1. Heat oil in pan, add onions, sauté until golden brown (8-10 minutes)
2. Add ginger-garlic paste, sauté for 1 minute
3. Add tomato puree, cook until oil separates (7-8 minutes)
4. Add red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder
5. Cook for 1-2 minutes
6. Add cashew paste, cook for 2-3 minutes
7. Add 1.5 cups water, bring to simmer
8. Add salt, cook for 5-6 minutes
9. Blend gravy with immersion blender until smooth (optional)
10. Return to heat, add garam masala and crushed kasuri methi
11. Add cream, mix gently, simmer for 2 minutes
12. Just before serving, add koftas to gravy (don't cook too long or they'll break)
13. Garnish with fresh coriander
14. Serve hot with naan or roti

NUTRITION (per serving - 3 koftas with gravy):
- Calories: 550
- Protein: 16g
- Carbohydrates: 42g
- Fiber: 6g
- Fat: 38g
- Calcium: 250mg
- Iron: 3mg

================================================================================
SECTION 3: RICE DISHES (15 recipes)
================================================================================

21. VEGETABLE BIRYANI (Fragrant Spiced Rice with Vegetables)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan option
Serving: 2 bowls
Prep time: 20 min
Cook time: 30 min
Total calories per serving: 450

INGREDIENTS:
- 1.5 cups basmati rice
- 2 cups mixed vegetables (carrots, beans, peas, cauliflower)
- 1 large onion, thinly sliced
- 2 tomatoes, chopped
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 green chilies, slit
- 1/4 cup yogurt (or 2 tbsp cashew paste for vegan)
- 1/4 cup mint leaves
- 1/4 cup coriander leaves
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 bay leaf
- 4 green cardamoms
- 4 cloves
- 1 inch cinnamon
- 1 star anise
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp biryani masala
- 2 tbsp ghee or oil
- Salt to taste
- 2.5 cups hot water
- Saffron strands soaked in 2 tbsp warm milk (optional)

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Wash basmati rice thoroughly 3-4 times until water runs clear
2. Soak rice in water for 30 minutes, then drain completely
3. Heat ghee in heavy-bottomed pan or pressure cooker
4. Add cumin seeds, bay leaf, cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon, star anise
5. Sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant
6. Add sliced onions, fry until golden brown (8-10 minutes)
7. Remove half the fried onions for garnish
8. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, sauté for 1 minute
9. Add chopped tomatoes, cook until soft (3-4 minutes)
10. Add mixed vegetables, sauté for 2 minutes
11. Add red chili powder, turmeric, biryani masala, salt
12. Cook masala for 2 minutes
13. Add yogurt (or cashew paste), mix well, cook for 2 minutes
14. Add mint and coriander leaves, mix
15. Add drained rice, gently mix with masala (don't break rice)
16. Sauté rice for 2-3 minutes until aromatic
17. Add 2.5 cups hot water (1:1.5 rice to water ratio)
18. Add salt if needed, bring to boil
19. Cover with tight lid, reduce heat to low
20. Cook for 12-15 minutes (if using pan) or 2 whistles (if using pressure cooker)
21. Do not open lid while cooking
22. Let rest for 10 minutes after cooking
23. Fluff rice gently with fork
24. Garnish with reserved fried onions and saffron milk (if using)
25. Serve hot with raita or salan

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 450
- Protein: 10g
- Carbohydrates: 75g
- Fiber: 6g
- Fat: 12g
- Iron: 3mg
- Calcium: 80mg

================================================================================
22. TOMATO RICE (South Indian Style)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-free
Serving: 2 bowls
Prep time: 10 min
Cook time: 20 min
Total calories per serving: 320

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup basmati rice
- 4 ripe tomatoes, pureed
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 green chilies, slit
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp chana dal
- 1 tsp urad dal
- 10-12 curry leaves
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder
- 2 tbsp oil or ghee
- Fresh coriander
- Salt to taste
- 2 cups water

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Cook rice with 2 cups water until fluffy (separate grains)
2. Spread cooked rice on plate to cool completely
3. Heat oil in pan, add mustard seeds, let them splutter
4. Add chana dal and urad dal, fry until golden (1 minute)
5. Add curry leaves and green chilies, sauté for 30 seconds
6. Add chopped onions, sauté until translucent (3-4 minutes)
7. Add ginger-garlic paste, sauté for 1 minute
8. Add tomato puree, cook until thick and oil separates (8-10 minutes)
9. Add red chili powder, turmeric, salt
10. Cook masala for 3-4 minutes until very thick
11. Add cooled rice to the masala
12. Mix gently until rice is evenly coated
13. Cook on low heat for 3-4 minutes
14. Garnish with fresh coriander
15. Serve hot with papad or raita

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 320
- Protein: 6g
- Carbohydrates: 52g
- Fiber: 4g
- Fat: 10g
- Iron: 3mg
- Vitamin C: 25mg

================================================================================
23. LEMON RICE (Chitranna)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-free
Serving: 2 bowls
Prep time: 10 min
Cook time: 10 min
Total calories per serving: 280

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup cooked rice (leftover or freshly cooked and cooled)
- 2 tbsp lemon juice (adjust to taste)
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp chana dal
- 1 tsp urad dal
- 1 tbsp peanuts
- 1 tsp turmeric powder
- 2 green chilies, slit
- 10-12 curry leaves
- 2 tbsp oil
- Fresh coriander
- Salt to taste

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Cook rice and spread on plate to cool (rice grains should be separate)
2. Heat oil in pan, add mustard seeds, let them splutter
3. Add chana dal and urad dal, fry until golden (1 minute)
4. Add peanuts, fry until crunchy (2 minutes)
5. Add green chilies and curry leaves, sauté for 30 seconds
6. Add turmeric powder, mix well
7. Turn off heat, let the tempering cool slightly
8. Add cooked rice to the pan
9. Add salt and lemon juice
10. Mix gently until rice is evenly yellow and coated
11. Garnish with fresh coriander
12. Serve at room temperature with papad or pickle

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 280
- Protein: 6g
- Carbohydrates: 42g
- Fiber: 3g
- Fat: 10g
- Iron: 2mg
- Vitamin C: 15mg

================================================================================
24. CURD RICE (Thayir Sadam - Yogurt Rice)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Gluten-free
Serving: 2 bowls
Prep time: 5 min
Cook time: 5 min
Total calories per serving: 250

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup cooked rice (slightly mushy)
- 1 cup yogurt (curd)
- 1/4 cup milk
- 1 tbsp pomegranate seeds (optional)
- 1 tbsp grated carrots (optional)
- 1 tbsp chopped grapes or apple (optional)

FOR TEMPERING:
- 1 tsp oil or ghee
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp urad dal
- 1/2 tsp ginger, finely chopped
- 2 green chilies, chopped
- 10-12 curry leaves
- Fresh coriander

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Cook rice with extra water so it becomes soft and slightly mushy
2. Let rice cool completely to room temperature
3. Mash rice slightly with back of spoon
4. Add yogurt and milk, mix well until smooth
5. Add salt and mix
6. Heat oil in small pan, add mustard seeds, let them splutter
7. Add urad dal, fry until golden (1 minute)
8. Add ginger, green chilies, curry leaves, sauté for 30 seconds
9. Add tempering to yogurt rice, mix well
10. Add fruits and vegetables if using
11. Garnish with fresh coriander
12. Serve chilled or at room temperature
13. Best served with pickle or papad

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 250
- Protein: 9g
- Carbohydrates: 38g
- Fiber: 2g
- Fat: 7g
- Calcium: 200mg
- Probiotics: Good source

================================================================================
25. COCONUT RICE
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-free
Serving: 2 bowls
Prep time: 10 min
Cook time: 10 min
Total calories per serving: 350

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup cooked rice (cooled)
- 1/2 cup fresh coconut, grated (or frozen)
- 1 tbsp chana dal
- 1 tbsp urad dal
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 2 tbsp cashew nuts
- 2 dry red chilies
- 10-12 curry leaves
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste
- Fresh coriander

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Cook rice and spread on plate to cool completely
2. Heat oil in pan, add mustard seeds, let them splutter
3. Add chana dal and urad dal, fry until golden (1-2 minutes)
4. Add cashew nuts, fry until golden (1 minute)
5. Add dry red chilies and curry leaves, sauté for 30 seconds
6. Add grated coconut, sauté on low heat until coconut is lightly roasted (3-4 minutes)
7. Add salt, mix well, turn off heat
8. Add cooled rice to the coconut mixture
9. Mix gently until rice is evenly coated
10. Garnish with fresh coriander
11. Serve warm or at room temperature with papad

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 350
- Protein: 6g
- Carbohydrates: 45g
- Fiber: 4g
- Fat: 17g
- Iron: 2mg

SECTION 4: BREADS (15 recipes)
================================================================================

26. PLAIN NAAN (Tandoori Flatbread)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian
Serving: 2 naans
Prep time: 2 hours (including rising)
Cook time: 10 min
Total calories per serving: 300

INGREDIENTS:
- 2 cups all-purpose flour (maida)
- 1/2 cup yogurt
- 1/2 tsp baking soda
- 1 tsp baking powder
- 1 tbsp sugar
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 2 tbsp oil or ghee
- Warm water as needed
- Butter for brushing

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. In large bowl, mix flour, baking soda, baking powder, sugar, salt
2. Add yogurt and oil, mix well
3. Add warm water gradually, knead into soft dough (8-10 minutes)
4. Dough should be soft and elastic (not too stiff)
5. Cover with damp cloth, rest in warm place for 2 hours (dough will double)
6. Punch down dough, divide into 4 equal balls
7. Roll each ball into oval or teardrop shape (1/4 inch thick)
8. Heat tawa or skillet on high heat
9. Place naan on hot tawa, cook for 30 seconds
10. Small bubbles will appear on surface
11. Flip tawa upside down over gas flame (or flip naan directly on flame)
12. Cook until brown spots appear (30-40 seconds)
13. Remove from flame, brush with butter immediately
14. Stack naans and keep covered to stay soft
15. Serve hot with curry

NUTRITION (per naan):
- Calories: 150
- Protein: 4g
- Carbohydrates: 28g
- Fiber: 1g
- Fat: 3g
- Calcium: 40mg

================================================================================
27. GARLIC NAAN
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian
Serving: 2 naans
Prep time: 2 hours (including rising)
Cook time: 10 min
Total calories per serving: 320

INGREDIENTS:
- Same dough as plain naan
- 6 cloves garlic, finely minced
- 2 tbsp fresh coriander, chopped
- 2 tbsp butter (for garlic butter)

GARLIC BUTTER:
- 2 tbsp butter, melted
- 2 tbsp minced garlic
- 1 tbsp fresh coriander

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Prepare naan dough as per plain naan recipe
2. After dough rises, divide into 4 balls
3. Roll each ball into oval shape
4. Sprinkle minced garlic on top, press gently into dough
5. Cook naan as per plain naan method
6. For garlic butter: Melt butter, add minced garlic and coriander
7. Brush hot naan generously with garlic butter
8. Sprinkle more fresh coriander on top
9. Serve immediately

NUTRITION (per naan):
- Calories: 160
- Protein: 4g
- Carbohydrates: 28g
- Fiber: 1g
- Fat: 4g

================================================================================
28. BUTTER NAAN
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian
Serving: 2 naans
Prep time: 2 hours
Cook time: 10 min
Total calories per serving: 400

INGREDIENTS:
- Same dough as plain naan
- 4 tbsp butter (melted)
- Fresh coriander

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Prepare naan dough as per plain naan recipe
2. Roll and cook naan as per plain naan method
3. Brush hot naan generously with melted butter
4. Apply butter multiple times for authentic taste
5. Sprinkle fresh coriander
6. Serve hot with butter chicken or dal makhani

NUTRITION (per naan):
- Calories: 200
- Protein: 4g
- Carbohydrates: 28g
- Fiber: 1g

29. ROTI (Whole Wheat Flatbread)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan
Serving: 2 rotis
Prep time: 15 min
Cook time: 10 min
Total calories per serving: 160

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup whole wheat flour (atta)
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1 tsp oil (optional)
- Warm water as needed
- Ghee or oil for brushing

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. In bowl, mix flour and salt
2. Add oil, rub into flour
3. Add warm water gradually, knead into soft dough (5-6 minutes)
4. Dough should be soft and pliable (not hard)
5. Cover and rest for 15-30 minutes
6. Divide dough into 6-8 small balls
7. Dust ball with dry flour, roll into 6-inch circle
8. Heat tawa on medium-high heat
9. Place rolled roti on hot tawa
10. Cook until small bubbles appear (30 seconds)
11. Flip roti, cook other side for 30 seconds
12. Using tongs, place roti directly on gas flame
13. Roti will puff up (5-10 seconds per side)
14. Remove from flame, brush with ghee or oil
15. Stack cooked rotis and keep covered in cloth

NUTRITION (per roti):
- Calories: 80
- Protein: 3g
- Carbohydrates: 15g
- Fiber: 2g
- Fat: 1g

================================================================================
30. PARATHA (Layered Flatbread)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian
Serving: 2 parathas
Prep time: 20 min
Cook time: 15 min
Total calories per serving: 280

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup whole wheat flour (atta)
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1 tbsp oil or ghee
- Warm water as needed
- Ghee or oil for roasting

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Mix flour, salt, and oil
2. Add warm water gradually, knead into soft dough (6-7 minutes)
3. Cover and rest for 15-20 minutes
4. Divide dough into 6 balls
5. Roll one ball into 4-inch circle
6. Apply ghee on surface, fold like fan (pleats)
7. Roll the pleated strip into a coil
8. Flatten coil, dust with flour
9. Roll into 6-7 inch circle
10. Heat tawa on medium heat
11. Place paratha on hot tawa, cook for 30 seconds
12. Flip, apply ghee on cooked side
13. Flip again, apply ghee on other side
14. Press with spatula to cook evenly (golden brown spots)
15. Paratha will have crispy, flaky layers
16. Serve hot with curry, pickle, or yogurt

NUTRITION (per paratha):
- Calories: 140
- Protein: 3g
- Carbohydrates: 22g
- Fiber: 3g
- Fat: 5g

================================================================================
31. PURi (Deep Fried Puffy Bread)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan
Serving: 2 puris
Prep time: 15 min
Cook time: 10 min
Total calories per serving: 220

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup whole wheat flour (atta)
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1 tsp oil
- 1/4 cup semolina (sooji) - optional for crispiness
- Warm water as needed
- Oil for deep frying

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Mix flour, semolina, salt, and oil
2. Add warm water gradually, knead into stiff dough (5-6 minutes)
3. Dough should be firmer than roti dough
4. Cover and rest for 15-20 minutes
5. Divide dough into 12-15 small balls
6. Roll each ball into 3-inch circle (not too thin)
7. Heat oil in kadai for deep frying (medium-high heat)
8. Test oil: Drop small dough piece - should rise slowly
9. Gently slide rolled puri into hot oil
10. Press puri with slotted spoon to help it puff
11. Puri will puff up in 3-5 seconds
12. Flip and cook other side until golden (10-15 seconds total)
13. Remove with slotted spoon, drain on paper towel
14. Serve immediately with aloo sabzi or chole

NUTRITION (per puri):
- Calories: 110
- Protein: 2g
- Carbohydrates: 16g
- Fiber: 2g
- Fat: 4g

================================================================================
32. BHATURA (Fermented Deep Fried Bread)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian
Serving: 2 bhaturas
Prep time: 4 hours (fermentation)
Cook time: 10 min
Total calories per serving: 320

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup all-purpose flour (maida)
- 1/4 cup semolina (sooji)
- 1/4 cup yogurt
- 1/2 tsp baking soda
- 1 tsp sugar
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1 tbsp oil
- Warm water as needed
- Oil for deep frying

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Mix flour, semolina, baking soda, sugar, salt
2. Add yogurt and oil, mix well
3. Add warm water gradually, knead into soft dough (8-10 minutes)
4. Dough will be sticky but soft
5. Cover and rest in warm place for 3-4 hours (dough will ferment and rise)
6. Do not knead again (handle gently)
7. Divide dough into 8-10 balls (don't press too much)
8. Roll each ball into 5-6 inch circle (thicker than puri)
9. Heat oil in kadai for deep frying (medium-high)
10. Oil should be hot but not smoking
11. Gently slide bhatura into oil
12. Press with slotted spoon to puff
13. Fry until golden brown on both sides (30-40 seconds total)
14. Remove, drain on paper towel
15. Serve immediately with chole (chickpea curry)

NUTRITION (per bhatura):
- Calories: 160
- Protein: 3g
- Carbohydrates: 28g
- Fiber: 1g
- Fat: 4g

================================================================================
33. MISSING ROTI (Maharashtrian Spiced Flatbread)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan
Serving: 2 rotis
Prep time: 15 min
Cook time: 15 min
Total calories per serving: 200

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup whole wheat flour
- 1/4 cup besan (chickpea flour)
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 1 green chili, chopped
- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder
- 1/2 tsp red chili powder
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1/2 tsp ajwain
- 2 tbsp fresh coriander
- Salt to taste
- Warm water as needed
- Oil for roasting

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. In bowl, mix wheat flour and besan
2. Add chopped onion, green chili, turmeric, red chili powder
3. Add cumin seeds, ajwain, coriander, salt
4. Mix all ingredients well
5. Add warm water gradually, knead into soft dough (5-6 minutes)
6. Cover and rest for 15 minutes
7. Divide dough into 6-8 balls
8. Roll each ball into 6-inch circle (may be slightly thicker than roti)
9. Heat tawa on medium heat
10. Place rolled roti on hot tawa
11. Cook until small bubbles appear (30 seconds)
12. Flip, cook other side for 30 seconds
13. Apply oil on both sides
14. Press with spatula until golden brown spots appear
15. Serve hot with yogurt or pickle

NUTRITION (per roti):
- Calories: 100
- Protein: 4g
- Carbohydrates: 18g
- Fiber: 3g
- Fat: 2g

SECTION 5: SNACKS & STREET FOOD (15 recipes)
================================================================================

34. SAMOSA (Fried Pastry with Potato Filling)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian
Serving: 2 samosas
Prep time: 30 min
Cook time: 20 min
Total calories per serving: 350

INGREDIENTS FOR DOUGH:
- 1 cup all-purpose flour (maida)
- 2 tbsp oil or ghee
- 1/2 tsp ajwain (carom seeds)
- 1/2 tsp salt
- Water as needed

INGREDIENTS FOR FILLING:
- 3 potatoes, boiled and mashed
- 1/2 cup green peas
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp fennel seeds
- 1 tsp ginger, grated
- 2 green chilies, chopped
- 1 tsp coriander powder
- 1/2 tsp garam masala
- 1/2 tsp amchur (dry mango powder)
- 1/2 tsp red chili powder
- Fresh coriander
- Salt to taste
- Oil for deep frying

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS FOR DOUGH:
1. Mix flour, ajwain, salt, and oil
2. Rub oil into flour until crumbly
3. Add water gradually, knead into stiff dough (5-6 minutes)
4. Cover and rest for 30 minutes

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS FOR FILLING:
1. Heat 1 tbsp oil, add cumin and fennel seeds
2. Add ginger and green chilies, sauté for 30 seconds
3. Add mashed potatoes, peas, and all spices
4. Cook for 3-4 minutes, add coriander
5. Filling should be dry (not wet)

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS FOR ASSEMBLY:
1. Divide dough into 8-10 balls
2. Roll each ball into 6-inch circle
3. Cut circle into two halves
4. Take one half, form into cone (overlap edges with water)
5. Fill cone with potato filling (1-2 tbsp)
6. Seal top edge with water to form triangle
7. Press edges to seal completely
8. Heat oil for deep frying (medium-low heat)
9. Fry samosas on low heat until golden and crispy (8-10 minutes)
10. Drain on paper towel
11. Serve hot with mint chutney and tamarind chutney

NUTRITION (per samosa):
- Calories: 175
- Protein: 3g
- Carbohydrates: 24g
- Fiber: 2g
- Fat: 8g

================================================================================
35. PANI PURI (Golgappa - Hollow Crispy Puri with Spiced Water)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan
Serving: 6 puris
Prep time: 30 min
Cook time: 15 min
Total calories per serving: 150

INGREDIENTS FOR PURI:
- 1 cup semolina (sooji)
- 2 tbsp all-purpose flour
- 1/4 tsp baking soda
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1 tbsp oil
- Warm water as needed
- Oil for deep frying

INGREDIENTS FOR PANI (Spiced Water):
- 1 cup fresh mint leaves
- 1 cup fresh coriander
- 2 green chilies
- 1 inch ginger
- 2 tbsp tamarind pulp
- 1 tsp cumin powder
- 1 tsp chaat masala
- 1 tsp black salt
- 1/2 tsp black pepper
- 1/2 tsp dry mango powder
- 2 cups cold water

INGREDIENTS FOR FILLING:
- 2 potatoes, boiled and mashed
- 1/2 cup boiled chickpeas
- 1 tsp chaat masala
- Salt to taste

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS FOR PURI:
1. Mix semolina, flour, baking soda, salt, and oil
2. Add warm water gradually to make stiff dough (knead 5 minutes)
3. Cover with wet cloth, rest for 20-30 minutes
4. Knead again for 2 minutes, dough should be smooth
5. Divide dough into very small balls (smaller than marble)
6. Roll each ball into thin circle (2-inch diameter)
7. Heat oil for deep frying (medium-high)
8. Drop rolled puri into hot oil, press gently with slotted spoon
9. Puri will puff up in 2-3 seconds
10. Fry until golden and crispy (30 seconds total)
11. Remove, drain on paper towel

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS FOR PANI:
1. Blend mint, coriander, green chilies, ginger with little water
2. Strain to remove fiber (optional)
3. Add tamarind pulp, all spices, and cold water
4. Mix well, refrigerate until serving

ASSEMBLY:
1. Make small hole in each puri
2. Fill with mashed potato and chickpeas
3. Dip in spiced water
4. Eat immediately (puris become soggy quickly)

NUTRITION (per 6 puris with filling and pani):
- Calories: 150
- Protein: 4g
- Carbohydrates: 30g
- Fiber: 3g
- Fat: 3g

================================================================================
36. PAV BHAJI (Spicy Mixed Vegetable Mash with Bread)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan option
Serving: 2 pavs (bread rolls)
Prep time: 15 min
Cook time: 25 min
Total calories per serving: 350

INGREDIENTS FOR BHAJI:
- 2 potatoes, boiled and mashed
- 1/2 cup cauliflower florets
- 1/2 cup carrots, chopped
- 1/2 cup green peas
- 1/2 cup bell peppers, chopped
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 2 tomatoes, pureed
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 tbsp pav bhaji masala
- 1 tsp red chili powder
- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder
- 2 tbsp butter or oil
- Fresh coriander
- Lemon wedges
- Salt to taste

FOR SERVING:
- 4 pav (dinner rolls/bread)
- 2 tbsp butter
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 1 lemon

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Boil all vegetables (except potatoes) until soft (10 minutes)
2. Drain and mash vegetables coarsely
3. Heat butter in large pan or tawa
4. Add onions, sauté until golden (5-6 minutes)
5. Add ginger-garlic paste, sauté for 1 minute
6. Add tomato puree, cook until oil separates (4-5 minutes)
7. Add pav bhaji masala, red chili powder, turmeric
8. Cook masala for 2 minutes
9. Add mashed vegetables and mashed potatoes
10. Mix well, add 1 cup water
11. Simmer for 10-15 minutes, mashing frequently with masher
12. Bhaji should be thick and smooth
13. Add salt and butter, mix well
14. Heat separate tawa, toast pav with butter until golden
15. Serve bhaji hot with toasted pav
16. Garnish with chopped onion, coriander, and lemon wedge

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 350
- Protein: 10g
- Carbohydrates: 55g
- Fiber: 8g
- Fat: 12g
- Iron: 4mg

================================================================================
37. VADA PAV (Potato Fritter Sandwich)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian
Serving: 1 vada pav
Prep time: 20 min
Cook time: 20 min
Total calories per serving: 320

INGREDIENTS FOR VADA (Fritter):
- 3 potatoes, boiled and mashed
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp turmeric powder
- 2 green chilies, chopped
- 1 inch ginger, grated
- 10-12 curry leaves
- Fresh coriander
- 1 cup besan (chickpea flour)
- 1/4 tsp baking soda
- Salt to taste
- Oil for deep frying

OTHER INGREDIENTS:
- 4 pav (dinner rolls)
- Dry garlic chutney (made with dry coconut, garlic, red chilies)
- Green chutney (coriander-mint)
- Tamarind chutney (sweet)
- Oil for roasting

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS FOR VADA:
1. Heat 1 tbsp oil, add mustard seeds, let them splutter
2. Add curry leaves, green chilies, ginger, sauté for 30 seconds
3. Add turmeric and mashed potatoes, mix well
4. Add coriander and salt, cook for 2-3 minutes
5. Cool potato mixture, shape into 8-10 balls
6. Make besan batter: Mix besan, baking soda, salt, and water
7. Batter should be thick enough to coat potatoes
8. Heat oil for deep frying
9. Dip potato balls in batter, coat completely
10. Deep fry until golden brown (3-4 minutes)
11. Drain on paper towel

ASSEMBLY:
1. Slit pav horizontally (don't cut completely)
2. Apply dry garlic chutney on one side
3. Apply green chutney and tamarind chutney on other side
4. Place hot vada in between
5. Press gently
6. Roast on tawa with little oil until crispy
7. Serve hot with extra chutneys

NUTRITION (per vada pav):
- Calories: 320
- Protein: 8g
- Carbohydrates: 45g
- Fiber: 4g
- Fat: 12g

38. BHEL PURI (Puffed Rice Snack)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan
Serving: 1 bowl
Prep time: 10 min
Cook time: 0 min
Total calories per serving: 180

INGREDIENTS:
- 2 cups puffed rice (murmura)
- 1/4 cup sev (crispy chickpea noodles)
- 2 tbsp crushed puri (papdi)
- 1/4 cup finely chopped onion
- 1/4 cup finely chopped tomato
- 2 tbsp chopped coriander
- 2 tbsp tamarind chutney (sweet)
- 2 tbsp green chutney (spicy)
- 1 tsp chaat masala
- 1/2 tsp black salt
- 1/2 tsp red chili powder
- 1/4 cup boiled potato cubes (optional)
- 1/4 cup pomegranate seeds (optional)

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Ensure all ingredients are ready (assembly is quick)
2. In large bowl, add puffed rice
3. Add sev and crushed puri
4. Add onions, tomatoes, coriander, potato, pomegranate (if using)
5. Add chaat masala, black salt, red chili powder
6. Add tamarind chutney and green chutney
7. Toss everything together quickly (don't overmix)
8. Bhel should be crunchy, not soggy
9. Serve immediately in paper cone or bowl
10. Garnish with more sev and coriander
11. Eat within 5-10 minutes (gets soggy)

NUTRITION (per serving):
- Calories: 180
- Protein: 4g
- Carbohydrates: 35g
- Fiber: 3g
- Fat: 4g
- Iron: 5mg

39. DHOKLA (Steamed Chickpea Cake)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-free
Serving: 4 pieces
Prep time: 30 min (plus 30 min resting)
Cook time: 15 min
Total calories per serving: 150

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup besan (chickpea flour)
- 2 tbsp semolina (sooji)
- 1/2 cup yogurt (or 1/2 cup water + 1 tsp lemon juice for vegan)
- 1 tsp ginger-green chili paste
- 1 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp eno fruit salt (or baking soda + citric acid)
- 1 tbsp sugar
- Salt to taste
- 3/4 cup water

FOR TEMPERING:
- 1 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp sesame seeds
- 2 green chilies, slit
- 10-12 curry leaves
- Fresh coriander
- Grated coconut

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. In bowl, mix besan, semolina, yogurt, ginger-chili paste
2. Add turmeric, sugar, salt, and water
3. Whisk to smooth batter (no lumps)
4. Rest batter for 30 minutes
5. Add eno fruit salt just before steaming
6. Mix gently in one direction (batter will become frothy)
7. Grease thali (plate) or dhokla pan
8. Pour batter into greased pan (1 inch thick)
9. Steam for 12-15 minutes (steamer or pressure cooker without weight)
10. Check doneness: Insert knife - should come out clean
11. Let cool for 5 minutes
12. Cut into diamond or square pieces
13. Heat oil for tempering, add mustard seeds, let them splutter
14. Add sesame seeds, green chilies, curry leaves
15. Pour tempering over dhokla pieces
16. Garnish with coriander and coconut
17. Serve with green chutney

NUTRITION (per piece):
- Calories: 150
- Protein: 6g
- Carbohydrates: 18g
- Fiber: 4g
- Fat: 6g
- Iron: 2mg

================================================================================
40. ALOO TIKKI (Potato Cutlets)
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan option
Serving: 2 tikkis
Prep time: 15 min
Cook time: 15 min
Total calories per serving: 200

INGREDIENTS:
- 3 potatoes, boiled and mashed
- 1/2 cup bread crumbs
- 1 tsp ginger-ginger paste
- 2 green chilies, chopped
- 1/2 tsp red chili powder
- 1/2 tsp chaat masala
- 1/2 tsp garam masala
- 1/4 cup corn flour
- Fresh coriander
- Salt to taste
- Oil for shallow frying

OPTIONAL FILLING:
- 1/2 cup boiled peas, mashed
- 1/4 cup paneer, grated (for non-vegan)

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Mix mashed potatoes with all spices, coriander, and salt
2. Add corn flour and half the bread crumbs
3. Mix well to form dough-like mixture
4. Divide into 8-10 equal portions
5. If using filling: flatten each portion, place filling in center
6. Shape into round patties (tikkis)
7. Coat with remaining bread crumbs
8. Refrigerate for 15 minutes (helps hold shape)
9. Heat oil on tawa for shallow frying
10. Place tikkis on hot tawa
11. Cook on medium heat until golden brown (3-4 minutes)
12. Flip carefully, cook other side until golden (3-4 minutes)
13. Press gently with spatula for even browning
14. Serve hot with mint chutney and tamarind chutney

NUTRITION (per tikki):
- Calories: 100
- Protein: 3g
- Carbohydrates: 18g
- Fiber: 2g
- Fat: 2g
