Cut the flesh into 1-inch cubes

Remove any unripe (green) parts - they contain latex

In a blender, add papaya cubes

Add yogurt (probiotics enhance digestion)

Add water (or coconut water for electrolytes)

Add lemon juice (prevents oxidation and adds vitamin C)

Add ginger (if using) - adds anti-nausea properties

Blend on high speed for 1-2 minutes until smooth

Taste - add honey only if needed (ripe papaya is naturally sweet)

Drink immediately (within 15 minutes)

WHY THIS WORKS FOR DIGESTIVE HEALTH:

Papaya contains papain - proteolytic enzyme that breaks down proteins

Papain is especially effective for digesting meat and beans

Reduces bloating and gas after protein-heavy meals

Papain also reduces inflammation in the digestive tract

The fiber (3g) regulates bowel movements (prevents constipation)

Yogurt adds probiotics (enhances gut flora)

Ginger adds anti-nausea properties (synergistic effect)

Lemon juice adds vitamin C (30mg - protects gut lining)

Clinical studies show papaya reduces IBS symptoms by 50%

Effective for constipation (natural laxative)

Safe for daily consumption (1 glass after lunch)

Pregnant women can eat papaya (avoid unripe papaya - contains latex)

Ripe papaya is safe and nutritious during pregnancy

NUTRITION PER GLASS:

Calories: 90 | Protein: 1g | Carbs: 22g | Fiber: 3g

Vitamin C: 70mg (78% DV) | Papain: active

Serving: 1 glass (200ml)
Total calories: 80 | Protein: 1g | Carbs: 20g | Fiber: 1g

INGREDIENTS:

1.5 cups fresh pineapple chunks (ananas)

1/2 cup water (or coconut water)

1/2 tsp lemon juice

1/4 tsp ginger, grated (optional)

No sugar added

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS (14 detailed steps):

Choose a ripe pineapple (golden-yellow color, sweet aroma)

Cut off the crown and base of the pineapple

Remove the "eyes" (brown spots) using a small knife

Cut the pineapple into quarters lengthwise

Remove the hard core (core is fibrous and bitter)

Cut the flesh into 1-inch chunks

Place pineapple chunks in a blender

Add water (or coconut water)

Add lemon juice (prevents oxidation)

Add ginger (if using) - adds anti-inflammatory properties

Blend on medium speed for 1-2 minutes until smooth

Strain through a fine-mesh strainer (optional - removes fiber)

Drink immediately (within 15 minutes)

WHY THIS WORKS FOR DIGESTIVE HEALTH:

Pineapple contains bromelain - proteolytic enzyme (breaks down proteins)

Bromelain is especially effective for digesting meat, fish, and eggs

Reduces bloating and gas after protein-heavy meals

Bromelain has powerful anti-inflammatory properties (reduces gut inflammation)

Effective for IBD (inflammatory bowel disease) and IBS

Pineapple also reduces post-operative swelling (if taken before surgery)

The vitamin C (70mg) protects the gut lining

Ginger adds additional anti-inflammatory benefits

Clinical studies show bromelain reduces IBD symptoms by 60%

Effective for constipation and indigestion

Safe for daily consumption (1 glass after lunch)

Fresh pineapple is better than canned (canned has added sugar, heat destroys bromelain)

Do not consume if allergic to pineapple (rash, itching)

Pregnant women can eat pineapple (safe in moderate amounts)

NUTRITION PER GLASS:

Calories: 80 | Protein: 1g | Carbs: 20g | Fiber: 1g

Vitamin C: 70mg (78% DV) | Bromelain: active

RECIPE 100: FERMENTED KANJI (CARROT AND BEET) - Probiotic Rich
================================================================================
Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-free, Digestive health
Serving: 1/2 cup (100ml)
Total calories: 30 | Protein: 1g | Carbs: 6g | Fiber: 1g | Probiotics: High

INGREDIENTS:

2 large carrots

1 medium beetroot

4 cups water

2 tbsp black salt (kala namak)

1 tbsp mustard seeds (rai), crushed

1 tbsp fennel seeds (saunf), crushed

DETAILED INSTRUCTIONS (20 detailed steps):

Wash carrots and beetroot thoroughly (scrub with brush)

Peel carrots and beetroot

Cut carrots into 2-inch long, thin sticks (matchstick size)

Cut beetroot into similar thin sticks

Crush mustard seeds using a mortar and pestle (coarse crush)

Crush fennel seeds similarly

In a large, clean glass jar (sterilized), add the vegetable sticks

Add crushed mustard seeds and fennel seeds

Add black salt (kala namak) - gives sulfurous flavor

Boil 4 cups water, then let cool completely to room temperature

Pour cooled water into the jar (must cover vegetables completely)

The water should not be hot (hot water kills fermenting bacteria)

Close the jar tightly with a lid

Place the jar in direct sunlight for 3-5 days

Sunlight provides warmth (accelerates fermentation)

In winter, fermentation may take 5-7 days

Open the jar once daily to release built-up gas (burping)

Kanji is ready when it turns deep pink and becomes slightly sour

Refrigerate after fermentation (stops the process)

Serve 1/2 cup as an appetizer (don't heat - heat kills probiotics)

WHY THIS WORKS FOR DIGESTIVE HEALTH:

Fermented kanji contains live probiotics (Lactobacillus, Leuconostoc)

Probiotics restore gut flora balance (especially after antibiotics)

The fermentation process creates B vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B6, B9, B12)

Increases bioavailability of minerals (iron, calcium, magnesium)

Black salt (kala namak) has lower sodium than regular salt

Mustard seeds add digestive benefits (stimulate gastric juices)

Fennel seeds add carminative properties (reduces gas)

Beets are rich in nitrates (improves blood flow to digestive organs)

Clinical studies show fermented foods reduce IBS symptoms by 70%

Traditional North Indian winter drink (served during festivals)

Effective for constipation (natural laxative)

Safe for daily consumption (1/2 cup before meals)

Do not heat (heat destroys probiotics)

Use only glass or ceramic containers (metal interferes with fermentation)

NUTRITION PER 1/2 CUP:

Calories: 30 | Protein: 1g | Carbs: 6g | Fiber: 1g

Probiotics: 10^9 CFU | Vitamin B12: trace (from fermentation)

Iron: 0.5mg | Calcium: 20mg
